---
title: "Session 1: Exploring Data with AI"
subtitle: "Business Intelligence & AI"
author: "Kerry Back, Rice University"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, revealjs-style.scss]
    slide-number: c
    transition: fade
    navigation-mode: linear
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    margin: 0.05
    center: true
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script src="https://hypothes.is/embed.js" async></script>
---

## The Day AI Came for Software {.quote-slide}

::: {.quote-text}
"In February 2026, [AI agents]{.amber} wiped **$2 trillion** from software company valuations in a single week. Investors decided that AI can replace BI dashboards, workflow tools, reporting layers, and Tier 1 support."
:::

::: {.quote-source}
--- Bloomberg, February 2026
:::

---

## The Market Signal {.shrink}

::: {.stat-cards}
::: {.stat-card}
::: {.stat-number}
$2T
:::
::: {.stat-label}
Market cap erased from software companies
:::
:::
::: {.stat-card}
::: {.stat-number}
70%
:::
::: {.stat-label}
Of BI dashboards never viewed after creation
:::
:::
::: {.stat-card}
::: {.stat-number}
3 wks
:::
::: {.stat-label}
Average wait for a custom report from the BI team
:::
:::
:::

::: {.explainer}
The market is betting that [conversational data access]{.accent} will replace the tools you buy today. What does that mean for your company?
:::

---

## A Familiar Story {.section-divider}

---

## The Email Chain {.shrink}

::: {.highlight-box}
**Monday 9:14 AM** --- CEO to VP Sales: "Which customers buy from multiple divisions? I want the list by Wednesday."
:::

::: {.highlight-box}
**Monday 11:02 AM** --- VP Sales to BI Team: "Need a cross-division customer overlap report. CEO priority."
:::

::: {.highlight-box}
**Monday 3:30 PM** --- BI Team: "We can start next sprint. ETA: 3 weeks. We need to join Salesforce, Legacy CRM, and HubSpot --- different schemas, no common key."
:::

::: {.highlight-box}
**Monday 3:45 PM** --- CEO: "Three *weeks*?"
:::

---

## What If It Took 30 Seconds? {.centered-statement}

## "Which customers buy from multiple divisions?"

::: {.explainer}
Type the question. Get SQL, a chart, and a narrative --- instantly.
:::

---

## Dashboard vs. Chat {.shrink}

::: {.two-cards}
::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
Traditional Dashboard
:::

- Pre-built by analysts weeks ago
- Fixed filters and dimensions
- Can't answer questions it wasn't designed for
- New question = new ticket = new sprint
:::

::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
AI Chat Interface
:::

- Ask any question in plain English
- Agent writes SQL, runs it, returns results
- Generates charts and narrative on the fly
- Follow-up questions in seconds
:::
:::

---

## Today's Learning Objectives

::: {.info-box}
::: {.box-title}
By the end of this session, you will be able to:
:::

- Explain why AI agents are disrupting the BI software market --- and why it matters now
- Experience conversational data access vs. traditional dashboards
- Ask business questions in plain English and get instant answers with charts
:::

---

## The Business Lens {.quote-slide}

::: {.quote-text}
"If [anyone in your company]{.amber} could query data in plain English --- no SQL, no tickets, no dashboards --- what decisions get made faster? What questions finally get asked?"
:::

---

## Hands-On: Single-System Queries {.shrink}

::: {.step-flow}
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
1
:::
::: {.step-title}
Simple Lookup
:::
::: {.step-desc}
"What are our top 10 customers by revenue?"
:::
:::
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
2
:::
::: {.step-title}
Filtering
:::
::: {.step-desc}
"Show me open deals over $100K in the Energy division."
:::
:::
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
3
:::
::: {.step-title}
Aggregation
:::
::: {.step-desc}
"What is average deal size by sales rep this quarter?"
:::
:::
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
4
:::
::: {.step-title}
Analytical
:::
::: {.step-desc}
"Which reps are trending above quota? Show me a chart."
:::
:::
:::

::: {.explainer}
We'll progress from simple lookups to analytical questions. Each prompt hits a single system --- Salesforce pipeline, NetSuite financials, or Workday headcount.
:::

---

## Meet Meridian Corp {.shrink}

::: {.info-box}
::: {.box-title}
Your Simulation Environment
:::

- **Meridian Corp** --- a $500M industrial distributor
- **3 divisions:** Industrial (~$250M), Energy (~$150M), Safety (~$100M)
- **10 enterprise systems** --- CRMs, ERP, HRIS, ticketing, and more
- **41 database tables** across all systems
- **26,000+ rows** of realistic, interconnected data spanning 3 years
:::

---

## Meridian's Systems at a Glance {.shrink}

::: {.three-cards}
::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
Sales & CRM
:::

- Salesforce (Industrial)
- Legacy CRM (Energy)
- HubSpot (Safety)
:::
::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
Finance & Ops
:::

- NetSuite ERP
- Coupa Procurement
- SAP Concur Expenses
:::
::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
People & Support
:::

- Workday HRIS
- Zendesk Support
- Jira Projects
- SharePoint Docs
:::
:::

::: {.explainer}
Three divisions. Three CRMs. One company. This is the cross-system reality most enterprises face.
:::

---

## Try It Yourself {.section-divider}

---

## Guided Exploration {.shrink}

::: {.info-box}
::: {.box-title}
Sample Prompts to Try
:::

- "What was Meridian's total revenue last year, broken down by division?"
- "Who are the top 5 customers in the Industrial division by lifetime value?"
- "Show me headcount by department. Are any teams growing faster than revenue?"
- "What's our average days-to-close for deals over $200K?"
- "Plot monthly revenue trends for all three divisions on one chart."
:::

::: {.explainer}
Start with the prompts above, then ask your own questions. The goal is to feel the difference between *waiting for a report* and *having a conversation with your data*.
:::

---

## Breakout Discussion {.shrink}

::: {.two-cards}
::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
Reflect
:::

- What surprised you about the speed or quality of answers?
- What question did you try that worked unexpectedly well?
- What question stumped the agent --- and why?
:::

::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
Apply
:::

- What would this look like at your company?
- Who currently answers these questions --- and how long does it take?
- What decisions are delayed because the data isn't accessible?
:::
:::

---

## Before Next Session {.shrink}

::: {.info-box}
::: {.box-title}
Homework
:::

- Explore Meridian Corp data on your own --- try at least 5 new questions
- Attempt one question that requires data from **more than one system**
- Write down: What broke? What was hard? What would you need to make this work for real?
:::

::: {.explainer}
Wednesday's session: [Our Data Is Everywhere]{.accent} --- we tackle the cross-system problem head-on.
:::

---

## Key Takeaway {.quote-slide}

::: {.quote-text}
"The bottleneck was never the data. It was [access to the data]{.amber}. AI agents don't replace your analysts --- they give everyone else the ability to ask questions without waiting in line."
:::
